text: Section 8.01(b)(i). Termination Fee. In the event that this Agreement is terminated by Parent pursuant to Section 8.01(d)(ii) or by the Company pursuant to Section 8.01(c)(i), then the Company shall pay to Parent, by wire transfer of immediately available funds, a fee equal to $2,100,000,000 (the "Company Termination Fee"), it being understood that in no event shall the Company be required to pay the Company Termination Fee on more than one occasion. The Company shall pay the Company Termination Fee concurrently with such termination or, in the case of a termination by Parent pursuant to Section 8.01(d)(ii), within two (2) Business Days after the date of such termination.

extractions.extraction_text: the Company shall pay to Parent, by wire transfer of immediately available funds, a fee equal to $2,100,000,000 (the "Company Termination Fee")

attributes.conditions: Termination by Parent for superior proposal or Company breach

text: Section 8.01(c)(i). Reverse Termination Fee. In the event that this Agreement is terminated by the Company pursuant to Section 8.01(d)(i), then Parent shall pay to the Company, by wire transfer of immediately available funds, a fee equal to $4,200,000,000 (the "Parent Termination Fee"). The Parent Termination Fee shall be the sole and exclusive remedy of the Company, its Affiliates and their respective stockholders against Parent and Merger Sub and any of their Affiliates, equityholders, partners, members, officers, directors, employees, agents and representatives.

extractions.extraction_text: Parent shall pay to the Company, by wire transfer of immediately available funds, a fee equal to $4,200,000,000 (the "Parent Termination Fee")

attributes.conditions: Termination by Company for Parent breach of financing or regulatory obligations

text: Section 5.04. Regulatory Efforts. Each of Parent and the Company shall, and shall cause their respective Subsidiaries to, use their respective reasonable best efforts to (i) take, or cause to be taken, all actions and to do, or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or advisable to consummate the Merger as soon as practicable, including preparing and filing as promptly as practicable all documentation to effect all necessary notices, reports and other filings and to obtain as promptly as practicable all consents, registrations, approvals, permits and authorizations necessary or advisable to be obtained from any third party or any Governmental Authority including the expiration or termination of any applicable waiting period under the HSR Act, as well as any applicable foreign competition and foreign investment laws and regulations. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement shall require Parent or its Subsidiaries to (A) sell, divest, license, hold separate, or otherwise dispose of or encumber any assets, rights, products, licenses, operations or businesses of Parent, its Subsidiaries, or the Company.

extractions.extraction_text: use their respective reasonable best efforts to (i) take, or cause to be taken, all actions and to do, or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or advisable to consummate the Merger as soon as practicable, including preparing and filing as promptly as practicable all documentation to effect all necessary notices, reports and other filings and to obtain as promptly as practicable all consents, registrations, approvals, permits and authorizations necessary or advisable to be obtained from any third party or any Governmental Authority including the expiration or termination of any applicable waiting period under the HSR Act

attributes.conditions: Best efforts standard; no hell-or-high-water obligation — Parent explicitly not required to divest assets

text: "Material Adverse Effect" means any event, change, circumstance, development, occurrence or effect (any such item, an "Effect") that, individually or in the aggregate, has had or would reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on the business, results of operations, financial condition or assets of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole; provided, however, that no Effect arising out of, or resulting from, the following shall be taken into account in determining whether a Material Adverse Effect has occurred or would reasonably be expected to occur: (i) general economic conditions or conditions in global or domestic capital, credit, financial or securities markets; (ii) conditions or events affecting the industry or industries in which the Company and its Subsidiaries operate; (iii) changes in applicable Law or GAAP; (iv) geopolitical conditions, hostilities, acts of war, sabotage or terrorism or military actions or any escalation or worsening of any of the foregoing; (v) pandemics, epidemics or disease outbreaks (including COVID-19 or any variants thereof) or any related governmental responses; (vi) any failure by the Company to meet any projections or forecasts.

extractions.extraction_text: "Material Adverse Effect" means any event, change, circumstance, development, occurrence or effect (any such item, an "Effect") that, individually or in the aggregate, has had or would reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on the business, results of operations, financial condition or assets of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole; provided, however, that no Effect arising out of, or resulting from, the following shall be taken into account in determining whether a Material Adverse Effect has occurred or would reasonably be expected to occur: (i) general economic conditions or conditions in global or domestic capital, credit, financial or securities markets; (ii) conditions or events affecting the industry or industries in which the Company and its Subsidiaries operate; (iii) changes in applicable Law or GAAP; (iv) geopolitical conditions, hostilities, acts of war, sabotage or terrorism or military actions or any escalation or worsening of any of the foregoing; (v) pandemics, epidemics or disease outbreaks (including COVID-19 or any variants thereof) or any related governmental responses; (vi) any failure by the Company to meet any projections or forecasts.

attributes.conditions: Excludes: macro conditions, industry conditions, law changes, geopolitical events, pandemics (COVID-19 explicitly named), missed projections
